question: Based on the stacked bar chart showing quarterly performance indicators for Store X, which statement about overall performance is most appropriate?

options.A: Q1 has the best overall performance because it has the lowest electricity consumption and fewer customer comments.
options.B: Q4 has the best overall performance because it has the highest stacked total across all indicators.
options.C: Q3 has the best overall performance because its values for all indicators are closest to the yearly average.
options.D: The chart cannot be used to assess overall performance accurately because it stacks indicators with different units and meanings that cannot be meaningfully added together.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Store Visits, Electricity Consumption, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
